title: API Integration Layer
description: A backend integration concept for connecting services, normalizing data, and supporting reliable application features.
summary: A backend integration layer concept for connecting APIs, shaping data, and giving frontend applications a reliable contract.

sections.heading: Overview
body: This project explores a common backend need: connecting multiple services and exposing a cleaner interface for the application.
The focus is on reliability, clear data contracts, and predictable error handling.

sections.heading: Challenge
body: APIs from different services often return inconsistent structures, errors, and timing behavior.
Without a clear integration layer, frontend code becomes harder to maintain and product behavior becomes less predictable.

sections.heading: Approach
body: I structured the concept around typed request/response models, normalization, validation, and focused service modules.
The API layer is designed to keep frontend implementation simpler while preserving enough backend flexibility for future changes.

sections.heading: Outcome
body: The outcome is a backend direction that supports cleaner application development and easier debugging.
It shows how fullstack context helps connect product behavior with reliable server-side implementation.
